rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks only into the target component implementation if bindings are implementation-specific; use component type scope for port-level assumptions and guarantees.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL structural integrity by aligning AGREE annex placement with component type or implementation scope as defined in the source model.
Only reference ports, properties, or data types explicitly exposed in the AADL model—never infer or assume implicit visibility.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes must use the exact syntax: annex agree {** ... **};
Component type annexes declare assumptions and guarantees over ports; implementation annexes bind subcomponent-level variables.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as AGREE variables unless explicitly mapped to typed ports or constants.
Do not invent numeric bounds (e.g., x >= 0) unless explicitly required by the requirement text and supported by numeric AADL data types.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
